title: primula calderiana subsp. strumosa
intro: Primula calderiana subsp. strumosa, also known as the Calder's primrose, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Primula calderiana subsp. strumosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
